title: Earnings call transcript: Lemonade Q1 2026 beats expectations with revenue growth
text: Lemonade exceeded Wall Street expectations in Q1 2026. EPS: -$0.47 vs. forecast of -$0.57 (17.54% positive surprise). Revenue: $258 million vs. expected $251.5 million (2.58% beat). In-force premium (IFP): $1.33 billion, up 32% year-over-year. Revenue growth: 71% year-over-year. Gross profit: $100 million, up 159% YoY. Adjusted EBITDA loss: $17 million (improved 64% YoY). The company extended its streak of accelerating growth to 10 consecutive quarters. Pet insurance reached a milestone of $500 million in IFP, becoming the first product line to achieve this level. Customer base expanded 23% YoY with 158,000 new customers added in Q1. Management expects Q4 this year will be EBITDA positive, as will the full year of 2027. Full-year guidance projects 33% revenue growth with 61% growth forecasted for FY2026. The autonomous vehicle initiative showed strong early traction with conversion rates approximately 70% higher than average. Executives emphasized competitive advantages from decade-long AI integration rather than access to current AI tools alone.
